description: Local-first MCP server for agent memory kits — persistent semantic search, witnessed retrieval, durable receipts, governed authority decisions, and claim-ledger trust enrichment. Default build uses in-process Candle embeddings (no Ollama required). Supports hybrid BM25+vector search with RRF, contradiction detection via belief propagation, typed knowledge graph with causal/temporal/semantic/entity edges, factor-graph reasoning, topological void detection, community detection, lawful subtraction, and adaptive query routing. SQLite+FTS5 authoritative store with usearch vector sidecar. All data stays on the operator's machine — no cloud, no API keys, no telemetry. Runtime tool profiles (lean, standard, agent, full) control the exposed surface. Ships with first-class integrations for Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and Codex.
name: Semantic Memory MCP

prompts.name: sm-recall
prompts.description: Search semantic memory for prior context before asking the user. Returns a structured query plan with namespace filters, retrieval mode selection, and evidence-verification steps.

arguments.name: query
arguments.description: The search query — what you want to recall

prompts.name: sm-capture-fact
prompts.description: Store a durable, source-attributed fact in semantic memory. Guides namespace selection, evidence linking, idempotency, and sensitivity classification.

arguments.name: source
arguments.description: Source attribution (URL, file path, or reference)

prompts.name: sm-audit
prompts.description: Audit the semantic memory store for health: duplicates, contradictions, stale facts, graph gaps. Read-only phase produces a report; reconciliation requires user approval.

arguments.name: namespace
arguments.description: Optional namespace to focus the audit on

prompts.name: sm-explore-graph
prompts.description: Explore the knowledge graph around a topic: find related items, trace connections between two concepts, or discover community clusters.

arguments.name: topic
arguments.description: The topic or concept to explore

prompts.name: sm-maintenance
prompts.description: Run semantic-memory store maintenance: integrity check, FTS rebuild, vacuum, re-embedding, or index repair.

prompts.name: sm-supersede
prompts.description: Replace a stale or incorrect fact with a corrected version. Preserves history via a 'supersedes' graph edge; search auto-filters the old fact.

arguments.name: old_fact_id
arguments.description: The ID of the fact to supersede

arguments.name: reason
arguments.description: Why the old fact is being superseded

resources.name: retrieval-strategy
resources.description: Primary strategy for source-first semantic-memory recall, adaptive routing, graph hydration, contradiction checks, and evidence-backed retrospectives.

resources.name: memory-curator
resources.description: Audit and improve the semantic memory store: find duplicates, contradictions, stale facts, and graph gaps. Reconcile only after user approval.

resources.name: memory-maintenance
resources.description: Store-level maintenance: integrity check, FTS rebuild, vacuum, re-embedding, index repair, and process cleanup.

resources.name: knowledge-graph-explorer
resources.description: Explore the typed knowledge graph: related items, shortest paths between concepts, community clusters, and topological voids.

resources.name: memory-keeper
resources.description: Delegate heavy memory operations (audit, bulk ingest, contradiction sweep, dedup, edge population) to a focused subagent.

resources.name: pooled-memory-operations
resources.description: Multi-device memory mesh: identity/provenance, typed synchronization, per-device SQLite primaries and server replicas, sparse routed retrieval.

resources.name: standard-workflow
resources.description: The canonical workflow for using semantic-memory MCP tools: search first, verify against current sources, store durable facts, supersede stale ones.

resources.name: authority-model
resources.description: The governed authority model: recall authority vs assertion authority vs action authority.

tools.name: sm_add_evidence
tools.description: Add evidence to a claim. Creates an EvidenceBundle linking the evidence text to the claim. Returns the evidence bundle ID.

claim_id.description: The claim ID to add evidence to.

evidence_text.description: The evidence text supporting the claim.

tools.name: sm_add_fact
tools.description: Add a fact to the knowledge base. Embedded and indexed for semantic search. Returns fact ID and content digest.

evidence_refs.description: Evidence references supporting this fact (URLs, fact IDs, source paths).

extract_entities.description: When true, extract named entities via Ollama and link them as graph edges (opt-in)

idempotency_key.description:
Optional caller-provided idempotency key. Retries with the same key and
payload return the original fact; omit it for a distinct append.

memory_kind.description:
Memory kind classification: durable_fact, preference, project_state, instruction_policy,
correction, observation, episode_summary, skill_procedure, ephemeral_inference.
Default: durable_fact. Ephemeral inferences require evidence_refs to promote.

namespace.description: Namespace to store the fact in (e.g. "general", "research", "coding")

sensitivity.description:
Sensitivity class: public, internal, confidential, restricted.
Default: internal. Confidential/restricted facts are blocked from autocapture.

tools.name: sm_add_graph_edge
tools.description: Add a durable, typed graph edge between two nodes. Edge types: semantic, temporal, causal, entity. Idempotent — same edge returns existing ID.

EdgeType.description:
Edge type for graph edges. JSON Schema enum helps LLMs pick the
right value without guessing.

confidence.description: For causal edges: confidence (0.0-1.0). Ignored for other types.

cosine_similarity.description: For semantic edges: cosine similarity (0.0-1.0). Ignored for other types.

delta_secs.description: For temporal edges: time delta in seconds. Ignored for other types.

edge_type.description: Edge type: semantic, temporal, causal, or entity

evidence_ids.description: For causal edges: evidence IDs. Ignored for other types.

metadata.description: Optional metadata as a JSON object string

relation.description: For entity edges: relationship name (e.g. "mentions", "modifies"). Ignored for other types.

tools.name: sm_benchmark_trust
tools.description: Benchmark trust quality across search results. Runs multiple queries and measures the trust distribution (supported/partially_supported/unsupported/contradicted/heuristic_only/persisted_unjudged) of returned results. Shows what fraction of retrieved facts have claim-ledger backing vs unjudged.

query_count.description: Number of benchmark queries to run (default 10).

tools.name: sm_community
tools.description: Detect communities in the knowledge graph (Leiden-inspired). Returns community assignments, optional contradiction scans, and compression recommendations.

inputSchema.description:
Parameters for sm_community

MCP-001: edges field removed — edges are now loaded from the store
automatically. Configuration params (resolution, seed, contradictions,
importance_scores) remain caller-supplied.

contradictions.description: Optional contradiction pairs to scan within communities

importance_scores.description: Optional importance scores per item for community-aware compression recommendations

resolution.description: Resolution parameter for community detection (default 1.0). Higher values favor smaller communities.

summarize.description: When true, generate an LLM summary for each community via Ollama (opt-in)

inputSchema.title: CommunityParams

tools.name: sm_compact_claim_ledger
tools.description: Verify and checkpoint the claim ledger when configured entry/byte thresholds are exceeded. Defaults to dry_run=true. A successful rotation atomically activates a digest-verified snapshot and retained tail, emits a compaction receipt bound to the prior ledger head, and keeps bounded backups.

dry_run.description: Preview the verified compaction without writing. Defaults to true.

max_backups.description: Number of prior verified generations/backups to retain (default 3).

max_bytes.description: Compact only when the active tail exceeds this byte count (default 16 MiB).

max_entries.description: Compact only when the active tail exceeds this entry count (default 10,000).

retain_tail_entries.description: Minimum number of recent entries retained verbatim (default 256).

tools.name: sm_consolidate_facts
tools.description: Consolidate two near-duplicate facts into one. Merges their content, updates the kept fact, and supersedes the other with a 'consolidated with' edge. Use this to clean up duplicate knowledge.

keep_id.description: First fact ID (will be kept and updated with merged content).

merged_content.description: Optional merged content. If not provided, content from both facts will be concatenated.

supersede_id.description: Second fact ID (will be superseded by the kept fact).

tools.name: sm_create_claim
tools.description: Create a typed Claim from a semantic-memory fact. The claim gets a source-spanned provenance record from the fact's metadata. Returns the claim ID.

fact_id.description: The fact ID to create a claim from (with or without "fact:" prefix).

tools.name: sm_decide_action_authority
tools.description: Return the canonical typed origin-authority decision receipt for acting on a fact. The purpose is fixed to action; recall or assertion authority is not reused. This read-only decision surface never returns memory content or performs the action.

GovernedAccessPurposeParam.description:
Purpose values carried by a delegation/elevation lease. The MCP tool fixes
the decision purpose independently, so callers cannot substitute recall.

GovernedLeaseParams.description: Existing multi-principal delegation/elevation lease contract in MCP-safe fields.

GovernedNamespaceScopeParams.description: Exact namespace/resource scope used by governed authority decisions.

inputSchema.description: Multi-principal request for a fixed-purpose assertion or action decision.
inputSchema.title: GovernedDecisionParams

tools.name: sm_decide_assertion_authority
tools.description: Return the canonical typed origin-authority decision receipt for asserting a fact. The purpose is fixed to assertion; recall authority is not reused. This read-only decision surface never returns memory content.

GovernedAccessPurposeParam.description:
Purpose values carried by a delegation/elevation lease. The MCP tool fixes
the decision purpose independently, so callers cannot substitute recall.

GovernedLeaseParams.description: Existing multi-principal delegation/elevation lease contract in MCP-safe fields.

GovernedNamespaceScopeParams.description: Exact namespace/resource scope used by governed authority decisions.

inputSchema.description: Multi-principal request for a fixed-purpose assertion or action decision.
inputSchema.title: GovernedDecisionParams

tools.name: sm_decoder_analyze
tools.description: Detect contradictions in search results. Runs syndrome detection, computes corrections, and applies belief propagation to refine confidence scores.

tools.name: sm_delete_fact
tools.description: Forget a single fact by id through the governed dependency-closure path. This build fails closed because no trusted authenticated authority issuer is injected into the MCP composition root.

fact_id.description: The fact id to permanently delete (bare UUID or prefixed "fact:<uuid>").

tools.name: sm_delete_namespace
tools.description: Permanently delete ALL memory in a namespace — facts, documents, chunks, sessions/messages. HARD delete, irreversible. Returns per-surface deletion count.

namespace.description: The namespace to permanently delete (all its facts, documents, chunks, and namespaced sessions).

tools.name: sm_detect_contradictions
tools.description: Detect contradictions among the top results for a query from their CONTENT (numeric, value, negation, or antonym disagreement) — no pre-asserted edges required. Returns candidate conflicting pairs, each with the signals that fired and a human-readable reason. Persist a confirmed pair with sm_add_graph_edge(edge_type="contradicts") so the decoder/community/factor-graph tools pick it up.

query.description: The query whose top results are scanned for content contradictions

record_to_ledger.description:
When true, record each detected contradiction pair to the claim-ledger
as a hash-chained ContradictionCandidate entry (requires the
`claim-integration` feature). Default false.

top_k.description: How many top results to scan (default 10)

tools.name: sm_discord_search
tools.description: Second-order retrieval: find items related to your search results through the graph, but NOT themselves direct hits. Loads edges from store automatically.

inputSchema.description:
Parameters for sm_discord_search

MCP-001: graph_edges field removed — edges are now loaded from the store
automatically. Caller supplies only the direct result IDs.

direct_result_ids.description: Direct result item IDs (the top-K from search)

inputSchema.title: DiscordSearchParams

tools.name: sm_embeddings_are_dirty
tools.description: Check if embeddings need re-generation after a model change. Returns true if the embedding model or dimensions have changed since the last embedding was stored.

inputSchema.description: Parameters for sm_embeddings_are_dirty (no params needed, but struct for consistency)
inputSchema.title: EmbeddingsAreDirtyParams

tools.name: sm_factor_graph
tools.description: Run factor graph belief propagation on stored graph edges. Models all 4 edge types as factors. Returns unified confidence scores after convergence.

FactorGraphNodeInput.description: A node input for the factor graph.

initial_belief.description: Initial belief score (0.0-1.0, from provenance or search score)

inputSchema.description:
Parameters for sm_factor_graph

MCP-001: edges field removed — edges are now loaded from the store
automatically. Caller supplies only node initial beliefs and optional
config overrides.

nodes.description: Nodes with their initial belief scores

self_influence.description: How much the node's own initial belief matters vs neighbor influence (default 0.6)

inputSchema.title: FactorGraphParams

tools.name: sm_get_fact
tools.description: Fetch one fact by id (bare UUID or prefixed 'fact:<uuid>'). Returns full content, namespace, source, timestamps, and metadata.

fact_id.description: The fact id. Accepts a bare UUID or a prefixed id like "fact:<uuid>".

tools.name: sm_get_fact_neighbors
tools.description: Fetch a fact plus its graph neighbors WITH their content in one call. Hydrates neighbor facts for ids returned by graph tools.

item_id.description: The node id whose neighbors to fetch (bare UUID or prefixed "fact:<uuid>").

tools.name: sm_get_messages
tools.description: Get most recent messages from a session within a token budget (default 4000), chronological order. Returns role, content, timestamps.

max_tokens.description: Return the most recent messages fitting within this token budget (default 4000).

session_id.description: Session id to read messages from.

tools.name: sm_get_routing_policy
tools.description: Return the current persisted RL routing policy, including weights, training example count, and last update time.

tools.name: sm_get_search_receipt
tools.description: Load a durable search receipt by receipt/request ID. Returns the stored receipt with evaluation time, retrieval family, result IDs, and digests.

receipt_id.description: The receipt/request ID to look up.

tools.name: sm_graph_path
tools.description: Find shortest path between two items in the knowledge graph. Traverses all edge types. Returns node IDs with edge evidence per hop.

tools.name: sm_import_envelope
tools.description: Import a projection envelope atomically. All records are committed in a single transaction or the entire import is rolled back. Pass the envelope as a JSON string.

envelope_json.description: The import envelope as a JSON string.

tools.name: sm_import_status
tools.description: Check whether an envelope has already been imported. Returns import receipts for the given envelope ID.

envelope_id.description: The envelope ID to check import status for.

tools.name: sm_ingest_document
tools.description: Ingest a document with automatic chunking. Splits into chunks, each embedded and indexed. Returns document ID and chunk count.

namespace.description: Namespace to store the document in

tools.name: sm_invalidate_graph_edge
tools.description: Invalidate a stored graph edge by ID. Append-only — edge is never deleted, only marked invalidated with a reason.

tools.name: sm_judge_support
tools.description: Judge the support state of a claim. Creates a SupportJudgment (supported, unsupported, contested, or heuristic_only) with optional rationale.

tools.name: sm_list_facts
tools.description: Enumerate facts in a namespace (newest first) with pagination. Exhaustive, not similarity-ranked — for browsing, auditing, or deduping.

tools.name: sm_list_graph_edges
tools.description: List graph edges for a specific node (as source or target), or all edges if no node_id. Returns non-invalidated edges only.

limit.description: Maximum edges returned. Hard capped by the tool rail.

node_id.description: Node ID to list edges for. If omitted, lists all edges.

tools.name: sm_list_imports
tools.description: List recent imports, optionally filtered by namespace. Returns import receipt records.

limit.description: Maximum number of import records to return (default 20).

tools.name: sm_list_namespaces
tools.description: List namespaces that currently contain facts. Use before sm_list_facts to discover what is stored.

tools.name: sm_list_sessions
tools.description: List recent conversation sessions (newest first) with message counts.

tools.name: sm_parse_choice
tools.description: Parse a choice from raw LLM output given a list of valid options. Handles extra text, casing differences, and partial matches. Returns the matched option or an error.

raw_output.description: The raw LLM output text that should contain a choice.

tools.name: sm_parse_json
tools.description: Parse JSON from raw LLM output. Handles think blocks, markdown fences, trailing text, and common JSON errors without an additional LLM call. Returns the extracted JSON as a string.

raw_output.description: The raw LLM output text that should contain JSON (may include think blocks, markdown fences, trailing text).

tools.name: sm_parse_json_value
tools.description: Parse JSON from raw LLM output as an untyped serde_json::Value. Useful when the expected schema is unknown.

raw_output.description: The raw LLM output text that should contain JSON.

tools.name: sm_parse_number
tools.description: Parse a number from raw LLM output. Handles text like 'The answer is 42' or 'Score: 0.85'. Returns the number as a string.

raw_output.description: The raw LLM output text that should contain a number.

tools.name: sm_parse_string_list
tools.description: Parse a string list from raw LLM output. Handles markdown bullet lists, numbered lists, comma-separated values, and JSON arrays. Returns a JSON array of cleaned strings.

raw_output.description: The raw LLM output text that should contain a list of items.

tools.name: sm_query_claim_versions
tools.description: Query imported claim projection rows. Filters by scope, text, valid-time, and claim state. Returns claim version rows with full provenance.

inputSchema.description: Parameters for projection query tools (sm_query_claim_versions, etc.)
inputSchema.title: ProjectionQueryParams

tools.name: sm_query_entity_aliases
tools.description: Query imported entity-alias rows. Filters by scope, canonical entity, and text. Returns alias rows with merge and review state.

inputSchema.description: Parameters for projection query tools (sm_query_claim_versions, etc.)
inputSchema.title: ProjectionQueryParams

tools.name: sm_query_episodes
tools.description: Query imported episode projection rows. Filters by scope and text. Returns episode rows with cause/effect and outcome data.

inputSchema.description: Parameters for projection query tools (sm_query_claim_versions, etc.)
inputSchema.title: ProjectionQueryParams

tools.name: sm_query_evidence_refs
tools.description: Query imported evidence-reference rows. Filters by scope, claim, and claim version. Returns evidence reference rows with fetch handles and source authority.

inputSchema.description: Parameters for projection query tools (sm_query_claim_versions, etc.)
inputSchema.title: ProjectionQueryParams

tools.name: sm_query_relation_versions
tools.description: Query imported relation projection rows. Filters by scope, text, valid-time, and subject entity. Returns relation version rows with full provenance.

inputSchema.description: Parameters for projection query tools (sm_query_claim_versions, etc.)
inputSchema.title: ProjectionQueryParams

tools.name: sm_reconcile
tools.description: Reconcile detected integrity issues. Actions: report_only (just check), rebuild_fts (rebuild FTS indexes), re_embed (re-embed all content). Returns an integrity report after the action.

tools.name: sm_record_outcome
tools.description: MUTATING: record a caller-supplied proxy feedback label for retrieval routing. This is not a verified outcome. Persists the updated tabular routing policy every 10 outcomes.

outcome.description:
Caller-supplied proxy feedback label: "good", "bad", or "neutral".
This is training input, not a verified retrieval outcome.

query.description: The query string that was routed.

tools.name: sm_reembed_all
tools.description: Re-embed all facts, chunks, messages, and episodes. Call after changing embedding models. Returns the count of items re-embedded.

tools.name: sm_repair_json
tools.description: Attempt to repair common LLM JSON errors: trailing commas, unquoted keys, single quotes, missing brackets. Returns the repaired JSON string or an error.

json_string.description: The malformed JSON string to attempt repair on.

tools.name: sm_replay_search
tools.description: Replay a durable search receipt using query text and filters retained by explicit opt-in. Returns the replay comparison report.

inputSchema.description: Parameters for sm_replay_search using opt-in stored inputs.

receipt_id.description: The receipt/request ID whose stored inputs should be replayed.

inputSchema.title: ReplayStoredSearchParams

tools.name: sm_replay_search_receipt
tools.description: Replay a durable search receipt with caller-supplied query text and filters. Compares original results to replay results, reporting matches, missing IDs, and added IDs.

query.description: The query text to use for replay (receipts don't store query text).

top_k.description: Optional top_k for replay (defaults to original receipt's result count).

tools.name: sm_route_query
tools.description: Profile a query and get an adaptive routing decision. Determines which retrieval stages (BM25, vector, rerank, graph, decoder, discord) to activate.

query.description: The query string to profile and route

tools.name: sm_run_lifecycle
tools.description: Run a memory lifecycle pass: analyze items for syndromes, compute corrections, identify subtraction candidates, and check compression needs.

item_ids.description: Item IDs to process in the lifecycle pass

tools.name: sm_search
tools.description: Semantic hybrid search (BM25 + vector + RRF). Returns ranked results with content, scores, and stable result IDs.

namespaces.description: Optional namespace filter (restrict search to these namespaces)

top_k.description: Maximum number of results to return (default 5)

tools.name: sm_search_as_of
tools.description: Search facts that were valid (not superseded) as of a specific date. Uses bitemporal fields to filter results to only include facts that existed on the specified date.

as_of_date.description: The date to search as of (ISO 8601, e.g., "2026-01-15T00:00:00Z").

tools.name: sm_search_conversations
tools.description: Hybrid semantic search over stored conversation MESSAGES (not facts). Recall what was discussed in past sessions. Returns ranked messages.

tools.name: sm_search_proof_debt
tools.description: Search with proof-debt analysis. Runs a standard search, then for each result checks the claim-ledger trust index for support state. Returns results plus a proof_debt summary with total debt weight, unsupported count, and gate decision.

budget_micros.description: Proof-debt budget in micros for the gate check (default 500_000, one full proof unit).

namespaces.description: Optional namespace filter (restrict search to these namespaces).

top_k.description: Maximum number of results to return (default 5).

tools.name: sm_search_with_routing
tools.description: Adaptive search: profiles query, routes to appropriate stages, applies factor graph belief propagation if decoder is activated. Returns results with stable IDs.

group_by_community.description: When true, group search results by knowledge graph community membership

tools.name: sm_search_witnessed
tools.description: Mandatory witnessed retrieval. Bypasses cache, verifies durable receipt persistence, defaults to Current state, and supports privacy-preserving opt-in storage for complete replay.

ReplayModeParam.description: Opt-in retention policy for complete search replay.

inputSchema.description: Parameters for mandatory witnessed autonomous retrieval.

replay_mode.description: Replay input retention. Defaults to no_replay for privacy.

request_id.description: Optional caller correlation ID; generated when omitted.

retrieval_mode.description: Retrieval stage selection. Defaults to the current hybrid behavior.

inputSchema.title: SearchWitnessedParams

tools.name: sm_set_provenance
tools.description: Set provenance (evidence confidence) for an item. Confidence in [0.0, 1.0] with support count. Returns a provenance receipt.

item_id.description: The item ID to set provenance for

tools.name: sm_stats
tools.description: Get knowledge base statistics: fact/chunk/document/session counts, DB size, embedding model, and graph edge count.

tools.name: sm_strip_think_tags
tools.description: Strip </think> blocks from text. Removes chain-of-thought reasoning that some models emit. Returns cleaned text.

text.description: The text that may contain <think>...</think> blocks.

tools.name: sm_subgraph_prune
tools.description: Identify and optionally prune reasoning subgraphs in the knowledge graph. Runs autonomous subgraph maintenance: identifies connected subgraphs, ranks by access frequency (least-accessed first), and optionally prunes. Dry-run by default. Returns identified subgraphs, pruning priority, and pruning receipts.

dry_run.description:
When true, only identify subgraphs and report priority without pruning.
Default: true (dry run).

max_prune.description: Maximum number of subgraphs to prune (default: 5).

tools.name: sm_supersede_fact
tools.description: Create a replacement fact and link it to a stale fact via 'supersedes' edge. Use instead of deleting outdated facts. Returns new fact id and edge id.

namespace.description: Optional namespace for the replacement fact. Defaults to the old fact's namespace.

old_fact_id.description: Existing stale fact id. Accepts a bare UUID or prefixed "fact:<uuid>".

reason.description: Optional reason stored on the supersedes graph edge.

source.description: Optional source attribution for the replacement fact.

tools.name: sm_topology
tools.description: Find topological voids in the knowledge graph. Computes Betti numbers (components and cycles) and detects structural gaps. Loads edges from store.

inputSchema.description:
Parameters for sm_topology

MCP-001: edges field removed — edges are now loaded from the store
automatically. The params struct is kept for schema compatibility but
the edges field is no longer used.
inputSchema.title: TopologyParams

tools.name: sm_update_fact
tools.description: Update a fact's content in-place. Re-embeds the fact and updates FTS index. Use this to correct outdated facts without deleting and re-adding.

content.description: The new content for the fact.

fact_id.description: The fact ID to update (with or without "fact:" prefix).

tools.name: sm_vacuum
tools.description: Vacuum the database to reclaim space after deletions. This is a maintenance operation that may take a moment.

tools.name: sm_verify_claim
tools.description: Verify a claim against risk class requirements. Low/medium claims need cheap checks. High claims need falsification. Critical claims need replay AND falsification. Returns disposition: promote, reject, quarantine, or defer.

evidence_refs.description: Optional evidence references supporting the claim.

refutation_attempted.description: Whether refutation was attempted (if false, high/critical claims cannot be promoted).

risk_class.description: Risk class: low, medium, high, critical.

author: Josh Stevenson
